# List of Genetics Topics

Genetics is the study of the effect of genetic differences on organisms, often inferred by the absence of a normal component.
Geneticists study how traits are inherited across generations, how mutations alter phenotype, and how genes are organized on chromosomes.
Questions ask students to predict offspring ratios with Punnett squares, trace inheritance through pedigrees, calculate recombination frequencies, and apply Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium to populations.

## Topics

1. [Genetic Disorders](topic01/index.md) <span class='topic-count' title='6 questions'>6 questions</span> <a class="lt-link" href="https://bio.libretexts.org/Courses/Roosevelt_University/Advanced_Genetics:_Mechanisms_of_Inheritance_and_Analysis/01:_Foundations_of_Genetic_Disorders" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" aria-label="LibreTexts Chapter 1" title="Open on LibreTexts (new tab)"><img src="/assets/images/libretexts.png" alt="LibreTexts" class="lt-icon"><span class="lt-label">Chapter 1</span></a>
    - Students identify common genetic disorders and describe basic inheritance patterns associated with single-gene conditions.

2. [DNA Structure](topic02/index.md) <span class='topic-count' title='5 questions'>5 questions</span> <a class="lt-link" href="https://bio.libretexts.org/Courses/Roosevelt_University/Advanced_Genetics:_Mechanisms_of_Inheritance_and_Analysis/02:_DNA_Structure_and_Sequences" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" aria-label="LibreTexts Chapter 2" title="Open on LibreTexts (new tab)"><img src="/assets/images/libretexts.png" alt="LibreTexts" class="lt-icon"><span class="lt-label">Chapter 2</span></a>
    - Students answer questions about DNA composition, base pairing rules, nucleotide components, and double-helix complementarity.

3. [DNA Profiling](topic03/index.md) <span class='topic-count' title='18 questions'>18 questions</span> <a class="lt-link" href="https://bio.libretexts.org/Courses/Roosevelt_University/Advanced_Genetics:_Mechanisms_of_Inheritance_and_Analysis/03:_DNA_Profiling_and_Techniques" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" aria-label="LibreTexts Chapter 3" title="Open on LibreTexts (new tab)"><img src="/assets/images/libretexts.png" alt="LibreTexts" class="lt-icon"><span class="lt-label">Chapter 3</span></a>
    - Students use blood typing agglutination tests, HLA genotyping, gel electrophoresis band patterns, and DNA fingerprinting techniques to solve paternity disputes and forensic identification problems.

4. [Mendelian Genetics](topic04/index.md) <span class='topic-count' title='14 questions'>14 questions</span> <a class="lt-link" href="https://bio.libretexts.org/Courses/Roosevelt_University/Advanced_Genetics:_Mechanisms_of_Inheritance_and_Analysis/04:_Mendelian_Genetics" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" aria-label="LibreTexts Chapter 4" title="Open on LibreTexts (new tab)"><img src="/assets/images/libretexts.png" alt="LibreTexts" class="lt-icon"><span class="lt-label">Chapter 4</span></a>
    - Students predict offspring ratios using Punnett squares, apply Mendel's principles of segregation and independent assortment, and trace traits through pedigrees.

5. [Gene Interactions](topic05/index.md) <span class='topic-count' title='14 questions'>14 questions</span> <a class="lt-link" href="https://bio.libretexts.org/Courses/Roosevelt_University/Advanced_Genetics:_Mechanisms_of_Inheritance_and_Analysis/05:_Allele_and_Gene_Interactions" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" aria-label="LibreTexts Chapter 5" title="Open on LibreTexts (new tab)"><img src="/assets/images/libretexts.png" alt="LibreTexts" class="lt-icon"><span class="lt-label">Chapter 5</span></a>
    - Students determine how multiple genes interact to produce phenotypes, including epistasis in metabolic and biochemical pathways.

6. [Chromosomal Inheritance](topic06/index.md) <span class='topic-count' title='14 questions'>14 questions</span> <a class="lt-link" href="https://bio.libretexts.org/Courses/Roosevelt_University/Advanced_Genetics:_Mechanisms_of_Inheritance_and_Analysis/06:_Chromosomal_Inheritance" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" aria-label="LibreTexts Chapter 6" title="Open on LibreTexts (new tab)"><img src="/assets/images/libretexts.png" alt="LibreTexts" class="lt-icon"><span class="lt-label">Chapter 6</span></a>
    - Students trace inheritance of X-linked genes through meiosis, predict progeny outcomes from sex-linked crosses, and explain the chromosomal basis of inheritance.

7. [Chi Square Analysis](topic07/index.md) <span class='topic-count' title='9 questions'>9 questions</span> <a class="lt-link" href="https://bio.libretexts.org/Courses/Roosevelt_University/Advanced_Genetics:_Mechanisms_of_Inheritance_and_Analysis/07:_Chi_Square_Analysis" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" aria-label="LibreTexts Chapter 7" title="Open on LibreTexts (new tab)"><img src="/assets/images/libretexts.png" alt="LibreTexts" class="lt-icon"><span class="lt-label">Chapter 7</span></a>
    - Students perform chi-square tests on genetic cross data to evaluate whether observed ratios fit expected Mendelian predictions.

8. [Gene Mapping](topic08/index.md) <span class='topic-count' title='18 questions'>18 questions</span> <a class="lt-link" href="https://bio.libretexts.org/Courses/Roosevelt_University/Advanced_Genetics:_Mechanisms_of_Inheritance_and_Analysis/08:_Gene_Mapping_and_Recombination" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" aria-label="LibreTexts Chapter 8" title="Open on LibreTexts (new tab)"><img src="/assets/images/libretexts.png" alt="LibreTexts" class="lt-icon"><span class="lt-label">Chapter 8</span></a>
    - Students determine gene order on chromosomes using recombination data from test crosses, calculate map distances between linked genes, and use deletion mutants to establish gene arrangement.

9. [Chromosomal Disorders](topic09/index.md) <span class='topic-count' title='17 questions'>17 questions</span> <a class="lt-link" href="https://bio.libretexts.org/Courses/Roosevelt_University/Advanced_Genetics:_Mechanisms_of_Inheritance_and_Analysis/09:_Chromosomal_Disorders" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" aria-label="LibreTexts Chapter 9" title="Open on LibreTexts (new tab)"><img src="/assets/images/libretexts.png" alt="LibreTexts" class="lt-icon"><span class="lt-label">Chapter 9</span></a>
    - Students interpret karyotypes, identify aneuploid conditions, classify chromosomal abnormalities, and predict outcomes of meiosis with translocations.

10. [Population Genetics](topic10/index.md) <span class='topic-count' title='3 questions'>3 questions</span> <a class="lt-link" href="https://bio.libretexts.org/Courses/Roosevelt_University/Advanced_Genetics:_Mechanisms_of_Inheritance_and_Analysis/10:_Population_Genetics" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" aria-label="LibreTexts Chapter 10" title="Open on LibreTexts (new tab)"><img src="/assets/images/libretexts.png" alt="LibreTexts" class="lt-icon"><span class="lt-label">Chapter 10</span></a>
    - Students calculate allele and genotype frequencies in populations, apply the Hardy-Weinberg equation, and evaluate factors that change allele frequencies.

11. [Gene Trees](topic11/index.md) <span class='topic-count' title='12 questions'>12 questions</span> <a class="lt-link" href="https://bio.libretexts.org/Courses/Roosevelt_University/Advanced_Genetics:_Mechanisms_of_Inheritance_and_Analysis/11:_Gene_Trees_and_Relationships" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" aria-label="LibreTexts Chapter 11" title="Open on LibreTexts (new tab)"><img src="/assets/images/libretexts.png" alt="LibreTexts" class="lt-icon"><span class="lt-label">Chapter 11</span></a>
    - Students construct and compare phylogenetic trees, interpret evolutionary distances, and trace gene inheritance across species.
